The underlying AI service is an external endpoint and LLM responses should be treated as untrusted output. Developers should be aware of: Hallucination: LLMs may generate plausible-sounding but factually incorrect information. Do not treat LLM output as authoritative without verification. Indirect prompt injection: Data retrieved by tools, AI context providers, or chat history providers may contain adversarial content designed to influence LLM behavior or exfiltrate data through tool calls. Malicious payloads: LLM output may contain content that is harmful if rendered or executed without sanitization — for example, HTML/JavaScript for cross-site scripting, SQL for injection, or shell commands. Tool invocation: By default, all tools provided to the agent are invoked without user approval. The AI selects which functions to call and with what arguments. Function arguments should be treated as untrusted input. Developers should require explicit approval for tools with side effects, data sensitivity, or irreversibility. Developers should validate and sanitize LLM output before rendering it in HTML, executing it as code, using it in database queries, or passing it to any security-sensitive context. Apply defense-in-depth by combining tool approval requirements with output validation. Provides extension methods for to enable discoverability of .

108. 	/// <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient" /> to treat the conversation as service-managed — clearing
109. 	/// accumulated history between iterations and not injecting duplicate <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionCallContent" />
110. 	/// during approval-response processing.
111. 	/// </description>
112. 	/// </item>
113. 	/// <item>
114. 	/// <term>AI Service-stored chat history</term>
115. 	/// <description>

118. 	/// that intermediate ConversationId changes are captured immediately. For some services (e.g., the

120. 	/// call updates it anyway and updating the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentSession.ConversationId" /> has little effect

122. 	/// with each service call, so updating the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentSession.ConversationId" /> ensures that the
123. 	/// latest ID is always captured, even mid-run.

125. 	/// </description>
126. 	/// </item>

6. /// <summary>
7. /// Provides specialized run options for <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgent" /> instances, extending the base agent run options with chat-specific configuration.
8. /// </summary>
9. /// <remarks>
10. /// This class extends <see cref="T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions" /> to provide additional configuration options that are specific to
11. /// chat client agents, in particular <see cref="P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentRunOptions.ChatOptions" />.
12. /// </remarks>
13. public sealed class ChatClientAgentRunOptions : AgentRunOptions
14. {
15. 	/// <summary>
16. 	/// Gets or sets the chat options to apply to the agent invocation.
17. 	/// </summary>
18. 	/// <value>
19. 	/// Chat options that control various aspects of the chat client's behavior, such as temperature, max tokens,
20. 	/// tools, instructions, and other model-specific parameters. If <see langword="null" />, the agent's default
21. 	/// chat options will be used.
22. 	/// </value>
23. 	/// <remarks>
24. 	/// These options are specific to this invocation and will be combined with the agent's default chat options.
25. 	/// If both the agent and this run options specify the same option, the run options value typically takes precedence.
26. 	/// In the case of collections, like <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.Tools" />, the collections will be unioned.
27. 	/// </remarks>
28. 	public ChatOptions? ChatOptions { get; set; }
29. 
30. 	/// <summary>
31. 	/// Gets or sets a factory function that can replace (typically via decorators) the chat client on a per-request basis.
32. 	/// </summary>
33. 	/// <value>
34. 	/// A function that receives the agent's configured chat client and returns a potentially modified or entirely
35. 	/// different chat client to use for this specific invocation. If <see langword="null" />, the agent's default
36. 	/// chat client will be used without modification.
37. 	/// </value>

50. 	}
